steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes a chain: water evaporates from the crust $\Rightarrow$ voids form $\Rightarrow$ oil fills the voids $\Rightarrow$ fat content rises. The question asks what follows logically if less moisture is lost during frying.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the less the fat content is increased through frying.: less moisture lost means fewer voids, which means less oil absorbed — directly follows the passage's mechanism. ✓
  • Choice B — the higher the temperature must be to fry the food.: temperature is not part of the passage's moisture-void-fat chain. ✗
  • Choice C — the slower the crust will lose its permeability.: although the passage says water moves to the crust "as long as the crust remains permeable," it does not connect moisture loss to how slowly the crust loses permeability. ✗
  • Choice D — the crispier the crust will be when frying is completed.: reverses the expected outcome and introduces an unsupported link. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
